metadata.title: AI-Generated Code Vulnerability Tracker
metadata.description: Cataloging vulnerabilities specifically introduced or amplified by AI code generation tools
metadata.methodology: Vulnerabilities sourced from academic research, CVE databases, vendor security advisories, and independent audits. Minimum 10 CWE categories tracked.

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models frequently generate shell command constructions using unsanitized user input, especially in Python and Node.js
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Pearce et al., 'Asleep at the Keyboard? Assessing the Security of Code Generated by GitHub Copilot', IEEE S&P 2022
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Input validation wrappers; subprocess with shell=False; parameterized commands

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated database queries frequently use string concatenation instead of parameterized queries
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Sandoval et al., 'Lost at C: A User Study on the Security Implications of Large Language Model Code Assistants', USENIX Security 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Parameterized queries; ORM usage; SQL injection detection in SAST

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generate HTML rendering code without proper output encoding or Content Security Policy headers
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Tony et al., 'LLMSecEval: A Dataset of Natural Language Prompts for Security Evaluations', MSR 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Output encoding; CSP headers; DOMPurify for client-side rendering

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models trained on public repos reproduce hardcoded API keys, passwords, and tokens from training data
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Niu et al., 'CodexLeaks: Privacy Leaks from Code Generation Language Models in GitHub Copilot', USENIX Security 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Secret scanning in CI/CD; environment variable enforcement; git-secrets pre-commit hooks

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models suggest MD5, SHA-1, DES, or ECB mode encryption instead of modern alternatives
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Pearce et al., 'Asleep at the Keyboard?', IEEE S&P 2022
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Crypto linting rules; SAST rules blocking weak algorithms; library-level defaults

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated file handling code often lacks path canonicalization and directory boundary checks
vulnerability_categories.research_source: He & Vechev, 'Large Language Models for Code: Security Hardening and Adversarial Testing', CCS 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Path canonicalization; chroot/sandbox; allowlist-based file access

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models suggest pickle.load(), yaml.load(), and JSON deserialization without safe loaders
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Safe deserialization libraries; yaml.safe_load(); input validation before deserialization

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated C/C++ code frequently omits null checks after memory allocation or pointer operations
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Pearce et al., 'Asleep at the Keyboard?', IEEE S&P 2022
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Static analysis; smart pointers in C++; nullable annotations

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generate verbose error handling that exposes stack traces, database schemas, and internal paths
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Sandoval et al., 'Lost at C', USENIX Security 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Custom error handlers; structured logging; environment-aware error responses

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated API endpoints and route handlers frequently lack authorization middleware or access control checks
vulnerability_categories.research_source: He & Vechev, 'Large Language Models for Code', CCS 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Authorization middleware; RBAC frameworks; API gateway policies

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generating C/C++ code produce buffer operations without bounds checking
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Pearce et al., 'Asleep at the Keyboard?', IEEE S&P 2022
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Bounds checking; safe string functions; AddressSanitizer in CI

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated HTTP client code passes user-supplied URLs without validation or allowlisting
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: URL allowlisting; network segmentation; SSRF-specific WAF rules

aggregate_statistics.note: Prevalence scores based on frequency of vulnerable patterns in independent security evaluations of AI-generated code
